concerns.text: Tasks T013a (Motion Correction), T013b (Slice-Time/Normalization), and T013c (Smoothing) implement a full preprocessing pipeline using FSL/AFNI. This contradicts the Plan (Summary & Phase 1) and Spec (FR-002 context) which explicitly state that the 'Minimal Preprocessed' HCP release is used to satisfy preprocessing requirements, and that re-running these steps is 'unnecessary and computationally prohibitive'. Implementing these tasks constitutes a silent relaxation of the computational constraints (FR-007) and a deviation from the approved methodology (Plan) which relies on ICA-FIX denoised data. These tasks must be removed or re-scoped to 'QC only' to preserve the plan's constraint of running on a 7GB CI runner.

Task T028a introduces a 'GPU Escape Hatch' to run on Kaggle (device='cuda') if memory limits are exceeded. This violates the Spec's FR-007 which mandates execution on a 'GitHub Actions ubuntu-latest runner'. The Plan's 'GPU Escape Hatch' section is a methodological deviation that contradicts the Spec's hard constraint on the execution environment. A task implementing an unauthorized external dependency (Kaggle) weakens the Spec's requirement for a self-contained, reproducible CI pipeline.

Task T014a enforces a tSNR threshold filter but lacks a verification step to record the *actual* tSNR percentage or the count of subjects passing/failing. Spec SC-002 requires that preprocessing quality is 'measured against' the standard (tSNR ≥ 50 for ≥ 90% of voxels). Without a task to explicitly log/record these metrics (e.g., in a validation report or CSV), the acceptance criterion of 'measuring against' cannot be verified, effectively dropping the evidence requirement of SC-002.

Tasks T025a and T025b split FDR correction into two separate outputs (individual metrics vs. PCA factors). Spec FR-005 requires applying FDR to 'all correlation p-values' and reporting 'only those surviving'. The tasks do not include a step to merge or reconcile these two streams into a single, unified reportable result. This risks inconsistent reporting and fails to satisfy the requirement of a single, coherent statistical output for the final report, potentially dropping the 'report only those surviving' acceptance criterion for the combined set of tests.
